{{infobox football tournament season
|title           = Copa do Brasil
|year            = 2011
|other_titles    = Copa Kia do Brasil 2011
|image           = Copa-KIA-do-Brasil.jpg
|imagesize       = 250px
|caption         = 
|country         = Brazil
|dates           = February 16 - June 8
|num_teams       = 64
|champions       = {{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[CR Vasco da Gama|Vasco da Gama]]
|runner-up       = {{flagicon|Paraná}} [[Coritiba Foot Ball Club|Coritiba]]
|matches         = 111
|goals           = 315
|top goal scorer = [[Carlos Adriano Sousa Cruz|Adriano]]<br>[[Alecsandro]]<br>[[Rafael Coelho]]<br>[[Kléber de Souza Freitas|Kléber]]<br>[[William Salles de Lima Souza Júnior|William]]<br>(5 goals each)
|player          = 
|prevseason      = [[2010 Copa do Brasil|2010]]
|nextseason      = [[2012 Copa do Brasil|2012]]
}}

The '''2011 Copa do Brasil''' (officially known as the '''2011 Copa [[Kia Motors|Kia]] do Brasil''' for sponsorship reasons) was the 23rd edition of the [[Copa do Brasil]], Brazil's national football cup tournament. It began on February 16 and ended on June 8. This edition's champion was [[CR Vasco da Gama|Vasco da Gama]], with [[Coritiba Foot Ball Club|Coritiba]] ending as runners-up.
{{TOC left|limit=2}}

==Format==
The competition is a [[single elimination]] [[knockout tournament]] featuring [[two-legged tie]]s. In the first two rounds, if the away team wins the first match by 2 or more goals, the winner advances to the next round and the second leg will not be played. The [[away goals rule]] will be used. The winner qualifies to the [[2012 Copa Libertadores]].

==Qualified teams==
Sixty-four teams qualified to the 2011 Copa do Brasil either through their states championship or through a ranking of teams.

===Qualified by state championships and other competitions===
Fifty-four teams qualified via their respective state competitions. Depending on their status with the [[Brazilian Football Confederation]], each of the 27 state federations sent anywhere from one to three clubs.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www2.cbf.com.br/ranking/ranking_uf.pdf |title=CBF State Ranking – official site |access-date=November 17, 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091014095018/http://www.cbf.com.br/ranking/ranking_uf.pdf |archive-date=October 14, 2009 |url-status=dead |df=mdy-all }}</ref> Each state determined their own qualification criteria, but they usually sent the clubs with best records in the state championships or other special competitions.

===Qualified by CBF club ranking===
Ten clubs qualified as one of the top ten clubs in [[Brazilian Football Confederation|CBF]]'s club ranking, excluding those qualified by state competitions and clubs playing in the [[2011 Copa Libertadores]]. 
{| class="wikitable"
!width=28| Pos
!width=150| Team
!width=150| State
! Points <small>(2010)</small>
|-
|align=center| 3 || [[CR Vasco da Gama|Vasco da Gama]] || {{flag|Rio de Janeiro}} ||align=center| 2,086 
|-
|align=center| 5 || [[São Paulo FC|São Paulo]] || {{flag|São Paulo}} ||align=center| 2,049
|-
|align=center| 7 || [[Sociedade Esportiva Palmeiras|Palmeiras]] || {{flag|São Paulo}} ||align=center| 2,012
|-
|align=center| 13 || [[Goiás Esporte Clube|Goiás]] || {{flag|Goiás}} ||align=center| 1,523
|-
|align=center| 14 || [[Guarani Futebol Clube|Guarani]] || {{flag|São Paulo}} ||align=center| 1,516
|-
|align=center| 17 || [[Associação Portuguesa de Desportos|Portuguesa]] || {{flag|São Paulo}} ||align=center| 1,405
|-
|align=center| 22 || [[Santa Cruz Futebol Clube|Santa Cruz]] || {{flag|Pernambuco}} ||align=center| 1,140 
|-
|align=center| 23 || [[Paraná Clube|Paraná]] || {{flag|Paraná}} ||align=center| 1,080
|-
|align=center| 24 || [[Ceará Sporting Club|Ceará]] || {{flag|Ceará}} ||align=center| 1,056
|-
|align=center| 25 || [[Associação Atlética Ponte Preta|Ponte Preta]] || {{flag|São Paulo}} ||align=center| 1,047
|-
|}

|RD6-score01-2=3|RD6-score02-2=2
}}

==First phase==
The First Phase began on February 16 and ended on March 3.

=== Group 1===
{{football box collapsible
|date=February 16
|time=21:00 ([[UTC−03:00]])
|team1=[[Murici Futebol Clube|Murici]] {{flagicon|Alagoas}}
|score=0–3 
|report=[https://web.archive.org/web/20110706151958/http://www2.cbf.com.br/2011/copadobrasil/pdf/mufl160211s.pdf Report]
|team2={{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[Clube de Regatas do Flamengo|Flamengo]]
|goals1=
|goals2=[[Ronaldinho]] {{goal|66}}<br />[[Carlos Renato de Abreu|Renato]] {{goal|72}}<br />[[Guilherme Negueba]] {{goal|90}}
|stadium=[[Estádio Rei Pelé]]
|location=[[Maceió]]
|attendance=15,100
|referee=Jailson Macedo Freitas ([[Federação Bahiana de Futebol|Bahia]])
}}
''Flamengo advanced because they won by at least two goals as the visiting team in the first game.''

=== Group 2===
{{football box collapsible
|date=February 16
|time=20:00 ([[UTC−04:00]])
|team1=[[Nacional Fast Clube|Fast]] {{flagicon|Amazonas}}
|score=2–0
|report=[https://web.archive.org/web/20110706152008/http://www2.cbf.com.br/2011/copadobrasil/pdf/fafo160211s.pdf Report]
|team2={{flagicon|Ceará}} [[Fortaleza Esporte Clube|Fortaleza]]

|goals2=
|stadium=[[Estádio Roberto Simonsen|Estádio Roberto Simonsen (SESI)]]
|location=[[Manaus]]
|attendance=1,500
|referee=Arnoldo Vasconcelos Figarela ([[Federação de Futebol do Estado de Rondônia|Rondônia]])
}}

{{football box collapsible
|date=February 23
|time=21:00 ([[UTC−03:00]])
|team1=[[Fortaleza Esporte Clube|Fortaleza]] {{flagicon|Ceará}}
|score=4–1
|report=[https://web.archive.org/web/20110706152927/http://www2.cbf.com.br/2011/copadobrasil/pdf/fofa230211s.pdf Report]
|team2={{flagicon|Amazonas}} [[Nacional Fast Clube|Fast]]
|goals1=[[Reginaldo Mendes dos Santos Júnior|Reginaldo Jr.]] {{goal|1||84}}<br />[[Gilmak]] {{goal|66}}<br />[[Carlos Augusto Almeida da Silva|Guto]] {{goal|75}}
|goals2=Pedro Henrique {{goal|61}}
|stadium=[[Estádio Alcides Santos]]
|location=[[Fortaleza]]
|attendance=5,375
|referee=Suelson Diógenes de França Medeiros ([[Federação Norte-rio-grandense de Futebol|Rio Grande do Norte]])
}}
''Tied on points 3–3, Fortaleza advanced on greater goal difference.''

=== Group 3===
{{football box collapsible
|date=February 16
|time=20:00 ([[UTC−03:00]])
|team1=[[União Esporte Clube|União]] {{flagicon|Mato Grosso}}
|score=4–4

}}
''Tied on points 2–2 and equal on goal difference, Guarani advanced on away goals.''

=== Group 10===
{{football box collapsible

|location=[[Belém]]
|attendance=4,868

}}
''Paysandu advance on points 4–1.''

==Second phase==
The Second Phase began on March 16 and ended on April 6.

=== Group 33 ===

|date=March 16
|time=21:50 [[UTC−03:00]]

|location=[[Santo André, São Paulo|Santo André]]
|attendance=594

}}
''Tied on points 3–3, Santo André advanced on away goals.''

==Round of 16==
The Round of 16 began on April 13 and ended on April 27.

|location=[[Florianópolis]]
|attendance=7,209
|referee=Ricardo Marques Ribeiro ([[Federação Mineira de Futebol|Minas Gerais]])
}}
''Tied 2–2 on points, Avaí advanced on away goals.''

===Group 56===
{{football box collapsible
|date=April 20

|goals2=

''São Paulo advanced on points 6–0.''

==Quarterfinals==
The Quarterfinals began on May 4 and ended on May 12.

===Group 57===
{{football box collapsible
|date=May 5, 2011
|time=21:50  [[UTC−03:00]]

==Semifinals==
The Semifinals began on May 18 and ended on May 25.

==Finals==
{{main|2011 Copa do Brasil Finals}}
The Finals was played on June 1 and June 8.

===Group 63===
{{football box
|date=June 1, 2011
|time=21:50 [[UTC−03:00]]
|team1=[[CR Vasco da Gama|Vasco da Gama]] {{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}}
|score=1–0
|report=[https://web.archive.org/web/20120608164039/http://www2.cbf.com.br/sumulas/vaco010611s.pdf Report]
|team2={{flagicon|Paraná}} [[Coritiba Foot Ball Club|Coritiba]]
|goals1= [[Alecsandro]] {{goal|50}}
|goals2=
|stadium=[[Estádio São Januário]], [[Rio de Janeiro]]
|attendance=21,365
|referee=[[Paulo César de Oliveira]] ([[Federação Paulista de Futebol|São Paulo]])
}}
----
{{football box
|date=June 8, 2011
|time=21:50 [[UTC−03:00]]
|team1=[[Coritiba Foot Ball Club|Coritiba]] {{flagicon|Paraná}}
|score=3–2
|report=[https://web.archive.org/web/20120608164051/http://www2.cbf.com.br/sumulas/cova080611s.pdf Report]
|team2={{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[CR Vasco da Gama|Vasco da Gama]]
|goals1=[[Rosimar Amâncio|Bill]] {{goal|29}}<br>[[Davi Rodrigues de Jesus|Davi]] {{goal|44}}<br>[[Willian Farias]] {{goal|66}}
|goals2=[[Alecsandro]] {{goal|12}}<br>[[Éder Luís]] {{goal|57}}
|stadium=[[Estádio Couto Pereira|Estádio Major Antônio Couto Pereira]], [[Curitiba]]
|attendance=31,516
|referee=[[Sálvio Fagundes]] ([[Federação Paulista de Futebol|São Paulo]])
}}
''Tied on points 3–3, Vasco da Gama won on away goals.''

{{winners||Copa do Brasil<br>2011 champion|{{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro|size=100px}}<br>[[CR Vasco da Gama|Vasco da Gama]]|1st}}
